intro: Caprifolium leschenaultii, also known as the Leschenault's honeysuckle or Indian honeysuckle, is a plant species belonging to the family Caprifoliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Caprifolium leschenaultii include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and tonics, and is also used to make herbal teas, capsules, and extracts.
